patterns.name: macro-to-call-expr
patterns.description: Older srcml emitted foo(x)-style references at statement scope as <macro>; newer versions mark them as <expr><call>.

patterns.name: argument-expr-name-embed
patterns.description: Older srcml left argument contents bare; newer versions wrap them in <expr>, and bare identifiers additionally gain a <name> wrapper inside that <expr>. Bare 2- and 3-term flag-style expressions like `A | B` or `A | B | C` (using bitwise/logical/arithmetic ops) also get per-token <name>/<operator> markup.

patterns.name: argument-operator-embed
patterns.description: Bare operator-only argument (e.g. `^`, `^=`, `|`, `&&`) gains an <operator> wrapper inside the new <expr>.

patterns.name: argument-struct-name-embed
patterns.description: Bare `struct NAME` argument: the type name gains a <name> wrapper inside the new <expr>, while the `struct` keyword stays bare.

patterns.name: argument-bare-call-embed
patterns.description: Bare zero-arg call `NAME()` argument is marked up as <call><name>NAME</name><argument_list>()</argument_list></call> inside the new <expr>.

patterns.name: empty-self-closing-argument
patterns.description: Older srcml emitted empty macro arguments as <argument/>; newer versions omit the element entirely.
